pi_items.source: verifier.py checks if the handoff_folder exists based on export_result.sh parsing with re.compile(r'handoff', re.IGNORECASE)
verifier.py requires count >= 8 to pass the folder populated requirement for full 20 points
verifier.py requires distinct_list_count >= 3 to pass the source diversity requirement for full 15 points
verifier.py matches the outgoing email 'to' field against 'backup@techcorp.org'
verifier.py fetches subject_keywords from metadata and checks if ANY keyword is in the drafted subject line
Independent python analysis of the first 50 ham emails in the dataset confirmed these keywords all naturally appear in the email headers/subjects

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the SpamAssassin Public Corpus (real historical ham emails) injected into the user's Inbox by setup_task.sh. Python analysis of these 50 emails confirms they originate from at least 11 distinct mailing lists, explicitly validating the 'known_lists' metadata claims (sadev, ilug, zzzzteana, etc.) which are found within the headers. The verifier.py logic explicitly requires the creation of a 'handoff' folder, the curation of at least 8 emails from 3 or more distinct mailing lists, and an outgoing briefing email drafted to backup@techcorp.org matching specific subject keywords.
